questions.question: The course describes good communication as a two-way process. What does this mean?
options: It means only the most senior person present does the talking
It means giving clear instructions and then moving on to the next task
It means writing everything down so nothing has to be said aloud
It is not only about giving information but also about making sure each person has understood the other

questions.question: Which element of the communication cycle turns one-way talking into real communication?
options: Jargon
The sender
Feedback
A noisy room

questions.question: Which of the following is counted as verbal communication in the course material?
options: An open, relaxed posture
Written notes and messages
A genuine smile
Maintaining eye contact

questions.question: Which is recommended good practice for verbal communication in care?
options: Use abbreviations and jargon to save time
Use plain, respectful language and the person's preferred name
Speak as quickly as possible to get through the information
Use baby talk so you seem friendly

questions.question: Why does the course say you should allow pauses and silence when listening to someone?
options: Silence shows the person you are too busy to talk to them
It encourages the person to stop talking sooner
It gives you time to plan which task you will do next
Silence gives people time to think, especially if they find talking difficult or tiring

questions.question: Which kind of question is most likely to draw out more than a simple yes or no answer?
options: Closed questions answered with yes or no
Open questions beginning with 'what', 'how' or 'tell me'
Questions asked very quickly one after another
Questions that already suggest the answer you want

questions.question: A person who speaks a different first language struggles to follow an explanation full of unfamiliar words. This is an example of which type of barrier?
options: A physical and sensory barrier
A language barrier
An environmental barrier
An emotional barrier

questions.question: Anxiety, low mood, embarrassment and feeling rushed are examples of which type of communication barrier?
options: Language barriers
Physical and sensory barriers
Emotional barriers
Environmental barriers

questions.question: How does the course describe Makaton?
options: A written language used only by doctors
A translation app for foreign languages
Signs and symbols used alongside speech
A type of hearing aid battery

questions.question: A person who has both hearing and sight loss may communicate using touch, objects of reference or which of the following?
options: A loudspeaker system
A long written report
A communicator guide
Standing further away from them

questions.question: A person becomes angry during a difficult conversation. What does the course advise you to do?
options: Raise your voice to match theirs
Ignore the complaint and walk away without a word
Listen first, keep your tone low and steady, and try not to take the anger personally
Argue back firmly so they know you are right

questions.question: What does the 'need to know' principle of confidentiality mean?
options: You may share information freely with the person's neighbours
You keep all information to yourself, even from the care team
You may tell anyone who says that they need to know
You share information only with those directly involved in the person's care

questions.question: When someone's first language is not English, whom should you use to interpret?
options: Whoever in the room happens to speak the most languages
No one; carry on in English regardless
A family member, because they will always translate accurately
A professional interpreter or an approved translation service

questions.question: According to the course, who is the best source for learning how a person prefers to communicate?
options: Another person who has broadly similar needs
A standard routine used for everyone the same way
The person themselves, as they are the expert on their own needs
Your own first impressions of them

questions.question: Your words and your body language do not need to agree with each other.
options: True
False

questions.question: An aid such as a hearing aid only helps if it is available and in good working order when the person needs it.
options: True
False

questions.question: When using an interpreter, you should speak to the interpreter rather than to the person you are supporting.
options: True
False

questions.question: Confidentiality is not absolute; if someone is at risk of serious harm, you may have to share information to keep them or others safe.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of non-verbal communication? (Select all that apply)
options: Facial expression
A written handover note
Saying the person's preferred name aloud
Gestures
Personal space and posture

questions.question: Which of the following help when communicating with someone who has hearing loss? (Select all that apply)
options: Shout as loudly as you can
Cover your mouth while you speak
Speak clearly and be ready to write things down
Face them in good light
Reduce background noise

questions.question: Which of the following are good confidentiality practice? (Select all that apply)
options: Post updates about people you support on social media
Share information only on a need-to-know basis
Keep records locked away or password-protected
Seek the person's consent before sharing wherever you can
Discuss the people you support in corridors and lifts

questions.question: Which approaches does the course recommend when communicating with a person living with dementia? (Select all that apply)
options: Allow plenty of time to respond
Use a calm tone and gentle body language
Correct them harshly over small details
Rush them so the conversation is quicker
Keep language simple and ask one thing at a time

questions.question: For deaf people who use British Sign Language, a qualified ___ interpreter may be needed.
Under the ___ Information Standard, services must ask about, record and meet the communication and information needs of the people they support.
In the communication cycle, the person who has a message they want to share is called the ___.
